pre_game.message: Hi, I'm H. It's a bit surreal being in a room with everyone like this, isn't it? Are you feeling more nervous or excited about the prize pool?
Hey H, I'm C. It definitely feels a bit strange in here. I'm mostly excited, though—I’ve got my heart set on a dream trip to Japan, so I'm ready to get started. How about you?
Japan sounds like an incredible trip! I'm leaning more towards nervous—I’ve got a major loan payment coming up next month, so winning this would be a massive relief for me and my family.
That sounds incredibly stressful, H. I'm lucky to be in a stable position, but I'm still determined to win for my family and that dream trip. I hope it works out for both of us.

I appreciate that. Japan sounds like a dream, but for me, it’s really about getting my head above water. Do you think we’ll have to work together to get the prize, or is it every person for themselves?
That’s the big question. I’m hoping there’s a way for us to cooperate so everyone wins, but I guess we’ll have to see how the rules play out.
I’m with you on that. Cooperation would be the dream, though I’m definitely feeling the pressure to make sure I come out on top for that loan.
I hear you. That kind of pressure is heavy, but hopefully we can find a way to make it work for everyone once we know the rules.
I really hope so, C. It would be such a relief to find a way that works for both of us once we see those rules.
Absolutely. Let's keep our fingers crossed that the rules allow for a win-win. Good luck, H!
Thanks, C! I really hope we both get what we're after. Good luck with that Japan trip!

post_game.message: H, why did you take $15,000 when the fair share was $10,000? That extra $5,000 contributed to the pot running out for the final players.

I understand your perspective. When it was my turn, I didn't have full information regarding the total number of players or the exact size of the pot. I made a decision to secure a higher amount for myself based on that uncertainty, but I realize now that it directly contributed to the pot running out for those at the end of the sequence. It wasn't my intention to leave others with nothing, but I see how my choice impacted the group.
